items.title: CCTV offline during knife incident at youth detention centre

children.content: Security cameras at Ashley Youth Detention Centre were offline when a detainee threatened a staff member with a knife during a cooking workshop, documents reveal.

items.title: Private school denies discouraging parents from telling victims about deepfake incident

children.content: The principal of a Tasmanian private school denies claims by two parents that the school ever tried to discourage them from telling their daughters they had been identified in AI deepfake images.

image.altText: School Signage saying "The Friends School"

items.title: Water and sewerage bill increases on the way for Tasmanians

children.content: Water bills for Tasmanian households and small businesses will rise by an average of 5.7 per cent each year for the next four years.

image.altText: Closeup of water spraying out of a garden hose, held by a woman.

items.title: Music promoter charged with cocaine, cannabis offences

children.content: The former managing director of Hobart events company Vandemonian Touring appears in court charged with trafficking cocaine and possessing cannabis.

image.altText: Man leaning on glass door frame, smiling for photo

items.title: ASIC drops case against Liberty Bell Bay smelter as buyer sought

children.content: It was alleged Liberty Bell Bay's former owner, British billionaire Sanjeev Gupta's GFG Alliance, had failed to lodge financial returns for numerous years. The company then went into administration, and ASIC has dropped its legal case.

image.altText: A blue sign with a flame on it and the word 'LIBERTY' underneath, on the side of the road.

items.title: Crucial interest rate has broken from the global benchmark, costing us billions

children.content: The 10-Year Treasury bond, which the Commonwealth issues to raise money, has pushed past 5 per cent, raising the cost of paying off debt and suggesting inflation is not under control.

image.altText: pile of australian currency featuring 100 and 50 dollar notes

images.altText: People walk near a billboard featuring an image of Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei

articles.title: Australia in talks over latest US proposal to open Strait of Hormuz

children.content: Australia has begun discussions with the United States about joining the latest US proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, as the Trump administration tries to build an international coalition to restore freedom of navigation in the critical waterway.

image.altText: Three ships and a small dinghy on a body of water.

articles.title: 'Planning for a rainy day': How China is dealing with the global oil shock

children.content: China relies heavily on Middle Eastern oil imports, but experts say a double-insurance strategy appears to have helped the country absorb at least the initial shock after disruption to the Strait of Hormuz.

image.altText: An aerial view shows a crude oil tanker at an oil terminal.

children.content: Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei says Tehran will eliminate "the enemies' abuses of the waterway" under new management of the Strait of Hormuz. Follow live.

items.title: Why the Victorian Liberals can't afford to lose this weekend's by-election

items.title: 'Children have ended up on main roads', SA childcare regulator says

children.content: South Australia’s childcare centre watchdog says children have injured themselves or "ended up on main roads" due to poor supervision and that one in five services in the state do not meet national quality standards.

items.title: Fed-up residents 'leaving town' due to murky tap water
